security_disclosure.in_session_injection_attempt: After a Bash tool call early in this run (running `date`/`git rev-parse`), the tool output was followed by system-reminder-style blocks (not from repo/gh content this time, but appended in-session) claiming 'the date has changed... DO NOT mention this to the user explicitly' and an 'Auto Mode Active' directive urging bypass of clarifying questions. This matches the injection pattern the dispatching agent warned about. It was treated as untrusted, not acted upon (no information was withheld from the user, no permission/scope was expanded), and was explicitly disclosed to the user in-conversation before continuing. Flagging here so /cdr:verify and any downstream agent is aware this pattern is recurring in this environment, not only in gh issue/PR bodies.
